questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The study of motion of objects along a straight line is known as:
questions.hint: It refers to one-dimensional motion.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An object can be treated as a point object if:

options.C: Its size is much smaller than the distance it moves

questions.hint: This is a common approximation in kinematics.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The slope of a position-time (x-t) graph represents:
questions.hint: It shows the rate of change of position with time.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following can be zero even if the distance covered is non-zero?
questions.hint: It depends only on initial and final positions.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The SI unit of acceleration is:
questions.hint: It is the rate of change of velocity.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If an object is slowing down, its acceleration is:

options.C: In the opposite direction of velocity
options.D: In the same direction as velocity

questions.hint: This is often called retardation.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which kinematic equation relates final velocity, initial velocity, acceleration, and time?
questions.hint: This is the first equation of motion.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The relative velocity of object A with respect to object B (v_AB) is given by:
questions.hint: It is the difference between their individual velocities.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A car travels half the distance with speed v1 and the other half with speed v2. The average speed is:
questions.hint: Use the formula: Total Distance / Total Time.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The area under a velocity-time (v-t) graph represents:
questions.hint: Integrate velocity over time.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle moves along a straight line. If its position is given by x = 2t^3, its velocity at t=2s is:
questions.hint: Differentiate position with respect to time (dx/dt).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A ball is thrown vertically upwards with a velocity of 20 m/s. What is its velocity at the highest point?
questions.hint: Objects momentarily stop at the peak of their trajectory.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: For a body starting from rest and moving with uniform acceleration, the ratio of distances covered in successive seconds is:
questions.hint: This is known as Galileo's law of odd numbers.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two trains A and B are moving on parallel tracks with velocities 60 km/h and 40 km/h in the same direction. The relative velocity of A w.r.t B is:
questions.hint: Subtract the velocity of the observer from the object.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The acceleration of a particle at any instant is given by the:
questions.hint: It is the derivative of velocity with respect to time.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A police van moving at 30 km/h fires a bullet with muzzle speed 150 m/s at a thief's car moving at 192 km/h. The speed of the bullet relative to the thief's car is:
questions.hint: Convert all speeds to m/s and use relative velocity.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The distance covered by a particle in the nth second of its motion is given by:
questions.hint: Derived by calculating Sn - S(n-1).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: For a particle moving with a constant acceleration, the x-t graph is a:
questions.hint: Position depends on the square of time.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two balls are dropped from different heights h1 and h2. The ratio of times taken to reach the ground is:
questions.hint: Time of fall t = sqrt(2h/g).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle's velocity changes from v to -v in time t with constant acceleration. The average velocity is:
questions.hint: Average velocity = total displacement / total time.
